$comment: grocery-list screenshot traversal + Tier-2 outcome assertion. Generates qa/flows/mobile.yaml (run scripts/qa/compile-flow.mjs). Screenshot names match qa/screenshots.config.json sources. The 'share' shot is modal-heavy (options menu -> share); left for a follow-up once the seed run is proven. The add-item assertion pins the core action: a typed item that was absent before the add is visible (by its row accessibilityLabel) after it.

survival.$comment: T4 chaos net — state-survival subflow (compiles to qa/flows/state-survival.yaml, run in the full profile + nightly, NOT per-PR). Core mutating action = add an item; then REAL process death (killApp on Android, stopApp on iOS); then relaunch keeping state and re-open the list. The intent oracle: the item added before the kill is still on the list after it — the 'app killed mid-write' regime Josh reported. Reuses @added-item (Avocado); the leading assertNot proves it wasn't there to begin with, so the post-kill assert can only pass if the write persisted through the kill.

upgrade.$comment: T4 chaos net — upgrade/migration subflow (compiles to qa/flows/upgrade-write.yaml + upgrade-assert.yaml; run by scripts/qa/upgrade-test.mjs, full profile + nightly, NOT per-PR). The migration bug class Josh hits: install a HEAD build over months-old data and lose it. `write` runs on the LAST-RELEASED binary (fresh install -> QA seed + add Avocado, a datum NOT in the seed); the harness then installs HEAD OVER it (no uninstall) and runs `assert`, which re-opens the list and proves BOTH a seeded item (Bananas) AND the written item (Avocado) survived the upgrade. If HEAD reads the persisted store differently than the released build wrote it, Avocado is gone and the assert fails.
